tier_justification: Multiple work-units (4 behavioral fixes F1-F4 plus breadcrumb/comment corrections and test additions across 4 files) in one shared library sourced by 3 hook scripts, but no irreversible/outward surface (local hook logic, no deploy/migration/external send) and no dependency chain requiring full-suite pre+post surfaces — tier-1 predicate.

evals.assert: als_count_invocations on an existing-but-unreadable (chmod 000) transcript emits a real reason tag on stderr, not silent empty stdout with no breadcrumb
evals.cmd: T=$(mktemp); echo '{"type":"assistant"}' > "$T"; chmod 000 "$T"; source hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh; out=$(als_count_invocations "$T" 2>/tmp/e1.err); err=$(cat /tmp/e1.err); chmod 644 "$T"; rm -f "$T" /tmp/e1.err; [ -n "$err" ] && ! printf '%s' "$err" | grep -qE '^skipped_malformed=' && echo PASS || echo FAIL
evals.negative_control: The same command against the pre-fix origin/main version of loop_state_common.sh returns empty stderr for the identical fixture, proving the check can fail

evals.assert: als_stable_invocations never logs outcome=recovered with reason=none for an all-lines-malformed transcript; it must use a distinct reason (all_lines_malformed)
evals.cmd: T=$(mktemp); printf '{"type":"assistant", BROKEN1\n{"type":"assistant", BROKEN2\n' > "$T"; export CLAUDE_DISCIPLINE_LOG=$(mktemp); export CLAUDE_HOOK_MAX_ATTEMPTS=2 CLAUDE_HOOK_SLEEP_S=0.01; source hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh; als_stable_invocations "$T" >/dev/null; grep -c 'reason=none.*outcome=recovered' "$CLAUDE_DISCIPLINE_LOG"
evals.negative_control: Reverting als_count_invocations to the pre-fix version (single skipped_malformed breadcrumb, no all_lines_malformed reason) makes this grep return 1 (the false claim) instead of 0

evals.assert: Full hook test suite (hooks/scripts/tests/run_all.sh) passes with zero failures on the merged branch
evals.negative_control: Injecting a deliberate assertion break (e.g. reverting the als_count_invocations fix while keeping the new tests) causes run_all.sh to report FAILED suites

evals.assert: unregistered_loop_guard.test.sh is byte-identical to origin/main (invariant: must pass UNMODIFIED)
evals.negative_control: Any edit to that file makes the diff line count nonzero

evals.assert: Diff manifest is a subset of the 5 files named in the fix-forward brief (no evals.json or unrelated files committed)
evals.negative_control: Accidentally committing a working evals.json or a stray junk file would add an unlisted path to this output

evals.assert: als_count_invocations stdout contract unchanged: always empty or an integer, never a reason string leaking to stdout
evals.cmd: T=$(mktemp); chmod 000 "$T"; source hooks/scripts/lib/loop_state_common.sh; out=$(als_count_invocations "$T" 2>/dev/null); chmod 644 "$T"; rm -f "$T"; case "$out" in ''|*[!0-9]*) echo FAIL;; *) echo PASS;; esac
evals.negative_control: A hypothetical regression that echoes 'read_error' to stdout instead of stderr would fail this grep
